text:
Market today: The tech and chip sectors are seeing mixed signals. Strong positive momentum from Meta and a new chip IPO (SK Hynix) suggests underlying strength, but Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI highlights growing competitive friction and IP concerns in the AI space. This could introduce volatility for your concentrated tech book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-24.46%) and SPAL (-27.71%) are both leveraged ETFs with significant drawdowns, flagged as SELL/NEW. These need immediate attention, as margin amplifies these drops and leveraged products decay. SMH (-3.98%) is also in the red.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) and their stops. Also, monitor how the Apple/OpenAI news develops, as it could sway broader tech sentiment.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged positions while staying alert to evolving tech sector dynamics.
plays: Respect the stop on SOXL at 200 or consider trimming given its -24.46% loss and leveraged decay.
Address SPAL's -27.71% loss; ensure you respect its default stop at 21.38 due to its leveraged nature.
CRWD is a holding with positive PnL (+4.06%); scanner shows an ACCUMULATE setup (7/12) with entry 186.84-186.65 and stop 185.53. Consider adding if conviction is high.
